message:
Close franken_networkx-mh90 and open dl59 pajek NodeView follow-up

Flip franken_networkx-mh90 ("[B12] Add round-trip behavior parity
tests across core formats") to closed now that the behavior-
signature round-trip tests have landed, and record a fresh follow-up
ticket for the pajek compatibility gap that the new tests explicitly
skipped.

Beads transitions in this commit:

- franken_networkx-mh90 "[B12] Add round-trip behavior parity tests
  across core formats" — in_progress -> closed. closed_at is
  2026-04-16T21:54:16Z with close_reason "Completed". Implementation
  landed in `tests/python/test_round_trip_identity.py` via the
  networkx-backed `_behavior_signature` / `_reference_graph` helpers
  and the new `test_round_trip_behavior_matches_networkx` matrix
  across gml/graphml/json/edgelist/adjlist for Graph / DiGraph /
  MultiGraph / MultiDiGraph.

- franken_networkx-dl59 "Pajek round-trip compatibility: support
  NetworkX writer expectations or document the gap" — new, status:
  open, priority 2 (P2 / medium), type: bug. Description captures
  the known gap exactly: `test_round_trip_identity.py` now
  explicitly `pytest.skip`s pajek because `fnx.write_pajek`
  delegates to `networkx.write_pajek`, which calls
  `G.nodes.get(...)` on the Python surface. FrankenNetworkX's
  NodeView doesn't expose a mapping-style `.get()` yet, so pajek
  writes can't currently flow through the NetworkX writer path.
  Follow-up options spelled out in the body: (a) add the missing
  NodeView compatibility method, or (b) route pajek writes through
  a compatible adapter — in either case re-enable the round-trip
  parity coverage.

Only the tracked `.beads/issues.jsonl` ledger moves; no Python,
Rust, or test file changes. `.beads/` is gitignored while the JSONL
itself is tracked, so the file is force-added — the standard
pattern for every prior beads commit in this repo.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"franken_networkx-deh","title":"Drawing & Visualization: draw_networkx variants, LaTeX export, network_text","description":"## Scope
Implement ~15 drawing/visualization functions:
- `draw_networkx` — main drawing function (matplotlib)
- `draw_networkx_nodes` — draw nodes only
- `draw_networkx_edges` — draw edges only
- `draw_networkx_labels` — draw node labels
- `draw_networkx_edge_labels` — draw edge labels
- `draw_circular` — draw with circular layout
- `draw_kamada_kawai` — draw with Kamada-Kawai layout
- `draw_planar` — draw with planar layout
- `draw_random` — draw with random positions
- `draw_shell` — draw with shell layout
- `draw_spectral` — draw with spectral layout
- `draw_spring` — draw with spring layout
- `draw_forceatlas2` — draw with ForceAtlas2 layout
- `write_latex` / `to_latex` / `to_latex_raw` — export graph to LaTeX/TikZ
- `network_text` — ASCII art representation of graph

## Implementation Approach
- Most draw_* functions delegate to matplotlib with layout computation
- Can wrap NetworkX drawing directly for many of these
- network_text: ASCII tree/graph rendering, pure Python
- LaTeX export: generate TikZ code, pure Python string generation

## Implementation Location
- Python: `python/franken_networkx/drawing/`

## Test Requirements
- Verify draw functions don't error on valid graphs
- Verify LaTeX output is valid TikZ
- Verify network_text produces readable output
- Visual regression tests (optional)","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:14:33.237335091Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T03:36:38.812894650Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T03:36:38.812214806Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["drawing","tier-4","visualization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_networkx-deh","depends_on_id":"franken_networkx-l85","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:16:02.736514279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

All pass parity tests. _to_nx count: 83 → 63.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-do1","title":"Traversal Extras: bfs_beam_edges, bfs/dfs_labeled_edges, generic_bfs_edges","description":"## Scope
Implement 4 advanced traversal functions:
- bfs_beam_edges(G, source, value, width=None) — BFS with beam search pruning. At each level, only expand the top-width nodes as ranked by value(node). Returns edges of the beam-search tree. Useful for exploring large graphs with a heuristic.
- bfs_labeled_edges(G, source, sort_neighbors=None) — yield (u, v, label) where label is one of 'tree' (tree edge in BFS tree), 'level' (edge to node at same level), 'forward' (edge to already-visited node at deeper level), 'reverse' (back edge). Uses BFS with level tracking.
- dfs_labeled_edges(G, source=None, depth_limit=None) — yield (u, v, label) where label is 'tree' (DFS tree edge), 'forward' (edge to descendant not via tree), 'back' (edge to ancestor), 'cross' (edge to node in different subtree), 'nontree' (any non-tree edge). Standard DFS classification.
- generic_bfs_edges(G, source, neighbors=None, depth_limit=None, sort_neighbors=None) — BFS with customizable neighbor function. neighbors(node) returns iterable of adjacent nodes, allowing filtered/weighted BFS.

## Implementation Strategy
- All are generators (yield edges as discovered)
- bfs_labeled: track discovery level per node, classify edges by level comparison
- dfs_labeled: track discovery/finish times, classify by back/forward/cross rules
- generic_bfs: same as bfs_edges but calls neighbors(node) instead of G.neighbors(node)
- bfs_beam: at each level, sort candidates by value(), keep top width

## Test Requirements
- Tree edges form a valid spanning tree (connected, n-1 edges)
- Back edges in DFS create cycles
- Forward edges go to deeper nodes, cross edges go to different subtrees
- bfs_beam with width=None equals regular BFS
- generic_bfs with default neighbors equals regular BFS
- NX cross-validation: same edge classifications on small directed graphs","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-22T17:34:37.942681144Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T22:38:06.367225188Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T22:38:06.366727547Z","close_reason":"Implemented 4 traversal functions: bfs_beam_edges, bfs_labeled_edges, dfs_labeled_edges, generic_bfs_edges.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["algorithms","traversal"]}

 {"id":"franken_networkx-ms8p","title":"PARAM: Implement create_using for graph generators","description":"## Problem
4 graph generators ignore create_using parameter:
- gnp_random_graph (generators.rs:341)
- watts_strogatz_graph (generators.rs:372)
- barabasi_albert_graph (generators.rs:391)
- another generator (generators.rs:409)

All always return Graph() regardless of create_using value.

## NetworkX behavior
create_using specifies return graph type: Graph (default), DiGraph, MultiGraph, etc.

## Implementation plan
In the Python binding:
1. Generate graph normally in Rust (returns Graph)
2. If create_using is DiGraph: convert by adding both directions for each edge
3. If create_using is MultiGraph: wrap in MultiGraph
4. If create_using is None/Graph: return as-is

## Testing requirements
Add to existing test files or create tests/python/test_create_using_parity.py:
- test_gnp_default_is_graph: type(gnp_random_graph(10, 0.5)) == Graph
- test_gnp_create_digraph: type(gnp_random_graph(10, 0.5, directed=True)) == DiGraph
- test_ws_create_digraph: watts_strogatz with create_using=DiGraph
- test_ba_create_digraph: barabasi_albert with create_using
- test_directed_has_both_directions: verify each undirected edge becomes 2 directed edges

Run: pytest tests/python/test_create_using_parity.py -v --tb=long

## Files to modify
- crates/fnx-python/src/generators.rs: handle create_using
- python/franken_networkx/__init__.py: may need wrapper logic
- tests/python/test_create_using_parity.py: new test file","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T00:17:27.308573046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-05T23:39:44.212648840Z","closed_at":"2026-04-05T23:39:44.212379580Z","close_reason":"gnp_random_graph already supports directed=True (returns DiGraph). The create_using parameter is a NX-specific pattern rarely used with random generators — watts_strogatz and barabasi_albert don't even support it in NX. The let _ = create_using in Rust is correct behavior for API compat.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
